design_decisions.d0r_pending_approval: D0.R made 1 assumption + 0 definition change(s) PROVISIONALLY (pending approval, direction-of-truth align/correct/discharge): ass:variance-nondegenerate

added_assumptions.label: ass:variance-nondegenerate
added_assumptions.statement: Along the sequence under study, the Horvitz-Thompson contrast is variance-regular: sigma_n^2 >= c_sigma / n.

proposed_from.topic: Design-based Wald inference for ego-cluster network experiments via exposure-incidence polynomials: finite-population HT exposure contrasts, closed first/joint exposure probabilities, and a dependency-graph CLT under bounded ego-overlap
proposed_from.novelty_justification: {"repo_axis":"The repository has no authored experimentation core for exp_ego_cluster_exposure_clt. The closest positive in-repo object is pid_network_exposure_v1, whose kernel is partial-identification under one shared hidden completion; it does not contain a design-based HT CLT, exact ego-cluster exposure algebra, or a Wald-coverage theorem. The negative lesson from panel_approx_nbhd_interference_v1 also matters: avoid high-level local-dependence tautologies and instead derive the dependency frontier from primitive overlap counts.","published_axis":"UganderKarrerBackstromKleinberg2013 compute exact probabilities only for selected exposure rules under graph-cluster randomization; Chin2018 assumes a restricted dependency graph rather than deriving one from ego supports; AronowSamii2017 leave both probability computation and local dependence at a higher level; LiuHuZhang2026 analyze ego-cluster designs but under a model-based framework; GaoDing2023 identify the covariance problem but do not solve a design-based ego-cluster variance envelope. No paper found in the scan combines signed-incidence exposure algebra, an overlap-derived dependency-degree frontier, and a finite-population HT oracle-Wald theorem for ego-cluster experiments."}
seed_list: Exact inclusion-exclusion formulas compute first and joint exposure probabilities for ego-cluster signed-incidence polynomials, not just selected neighborhood rules.
A Hall-type K_i=1 certificate decides when a joint ego-cluster exposure event has positive design probability under one shared incidence system.
There is a generic family of finite ego experiments where every marginal exposure propensity is locally feasible but the joint exposure endpoint is impossible under one shared completion.
A minimal rooted-feature basis separates count-aliased exposure laws for a bounded-degree ego-graph class.
Bounded ego-overlap and bounded incidence complexity imply a design-based HT CLT with oracle Wald coverage for ego-cluster exposure contrasts.
The same overlap primitives yield a self-normalized Hajek CLT once ratio linearization is controlled by exposure positivity.
Exact joint exposure probabilities permit a sharp conservative variance envelope frontier for ego-cluster Wald inference.
An ego-cluster design can be chosen to jointly optimize minimum exposure positivity and maximum dependency degree for exposure-polynomial contrasts.

the main design-based benchmark for exact first and joint exposure probabilities.","relevant_to":"prop:incidence-probabilities"},{"author":"Ugander, Karrer, Backstrom, and Kleinberg","year":2013,"venue":"KDD","bibkey":"UganderKarrerBackstromKleinberg2013","one_line":"Exact graph-cluster exposure-probability computation for selected network exposure conditions, serving as the closest exact-probability comparator.","relevant_to":"prop:incidence-probabilities"},{"author":"Chin","year":2018,"venue":"arXiv","bibkey":"Chin2018","one_line":"Stein-method CLT for randomized experiments under interference with restricted dependency-graph degree, providing the dependency-graph asymptotic template.","relevant_to":"thm:ego-overlap-clt"},{"author":"Chattopadhyay, Imai, and Zubizarreta","year":2023,"venue":"arXiv","bibkey":"ChattopadhyayImaiZubizarreta2023","one_line":"Design-based inference for generalized network experiments with stochastic interventions and conservative variance estimation, adjacent to but distinct from fixed exposure-label HT contrasts.","relevant_to":"oeq:conservative-variance"},{"author":"Gao and Ding","year":2023,"venue":"arXiv","bibkey":"GaoDing2023","one_line":"Shows regression-based network-robust covariance estimators can be anti-conservative, motivating a separate randomization-based conservative variance target.","relevant_to":"oeq:conservative-variance"},{"author":"Liu, Hu, and Zhang","year":2026,"venue":"arXiv","bibkey":"LiuHuZhang2026","one_line":"Closest ego-cluster design paper, but model-based rather than fixed-potential design-based HT inference.","relevant_to":"thm:ego-overlap-clt"}]}

seed_details.one_line: Exact inclusion-exclusion formulas compute first and joint exposure probabilities for ego-cluster signed-incidence polynomials, not just selected neighborhood rules.
seed_details.anchor: UganderKarrerBackstromKleinberg2013 plus AronowSamii2017 open the exposure-probability bottleneck; LiuHuZhang2026 confirms ego-cluster asymptotics still lack this design-based algebra.
seed_details.referee_objection: Immediate from inclusion-exclusion once the monomials are written down.
seed_details.rebuttal: The nontrivial part is the signed-literal bookkeeping for joint exposure events over shared ego-cluster indicators, which the published graph-cluster literature does not state in closed form.

seed_details.one_line: A Hall-type K_i=1 certificate decides when a joint ego-cluster exposure event has positive design probability under one shared incidence system.
seed_details.anchor: The K_i=1 strengthening path in the prior pid_network_exposure review narrows Conjecture 2 to matching or cover certificates before the capacitated case.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is just matching feasibility in disguise, with no causal content left.
seed_details.rebuttal: The causal content is the reduction from exposure-endpoint realizability to signed incidence feasibility and the exact translation of positive joint exposure support into Hall-type inequalities.

seed_details.one_line: There is a generic family of finite ego experiments where every marginal exposure propensity is locally feasible but the joint exposure endpoint is impossible under one shared completion.
seed_details.anchor: The prior pid_network_exposure witness request explicitly asks for a finite numerical example separating marginal feasibility from joint realizability.
seed_details.referee_objection: A four-unit witness is too small and too hand-tuned to matter.
seed_details.rebuttal: The credible version is not one witness but a parameterized open region of small designs with the same incompatibility inequality.

seed_details.one_line: A minimal rooted-feature basis separates count-aliased exposure laws for a bounded-degree ego-graph class.
seed_details.anchor: The failed aliasing proposal identifies the need for a theorem-level separator beyond the C6 versus 2K3 sanity witness.
seed_details.referee_objection: This collapses into a quotient definition of equivalence rather than a theorem with content.
seed_details.rebuttal: The only defensible version is a necessity-and-sufficiency basis theorem over a named graph class; otherwise the objection lands.

seed_details.one_line: Bounded ego-overlap and bounded incidence complexity imply a design-based HT CLT with oracle Wald coverage for ego-cluster exposure contrasts.
seed_details.anchor: Chin2018 and AronowSamii2017 motivate the dependency-graph route; the gaps file isolates the missing primitive translation into ego-overlap counts, and LiuHuZhang2026 provides the closest ego-cluster comparator from a different framework.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from Chin once you name the dependency graph.
seed_details.rebuttal: The kernel is precisely to derive that graph and its degree from observable exposure supports and then connect the HT weights to exact ego-cluster probabilities, not to assume the graph exogenously.

seed_details.one_line: The same overlap primitives yield a self-normalized Hajek CLT once ratio linearization is controlled by exposure positivity.
seed_details.anchor: GaoDing2023 make Hajek and weighted least squares central in network experiments, suggesting a ratio-based refinement after the HT limit law.
seed_details.referee_objection: After the HT CLT, the Hajek version is a routine delta-method corollary.
seed_details.rebuttal: That objection is partly right, which is why this seed scored below the HT kernel and was not chosen as the main angle.

seed_details.one_line: Exact joint exposure probabilities permit a sharp conservative variance envelope frontier for ego-cluster Wald inference.
seed_details.anchor: AronowSamii2017, Leung2019ANI, and GaoDing2023 together isolate the remaining obstacle after probability algebra: the unobserved cross-potential part of the design variance.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is just Neyman conservativeness rewritten for exposure mappings.
seed_details.rebuttal: The missing term depends on exposure-pair potential outcomes that are not jointly observed even when pi_ij is known, so the sharp envelope problem is genuinely different from classical two-arm Neyman variance.

seed_details.one_line: An ego-cluster design can be chosen to jointly optimize minimum exposure positivity and maximum dependency degree for exposure-polynomial contrasts.
seed_details.anchor: UganderKarrerBackstromKleinberg2013, Leung2021RateOptimal, VivianoLeiImbensKarrerSchrijversShi2023, and LiuHuZhang2026 all point to design choice as the remaining efficiency frontier.
seed_details.referee_objection: The objective is ad hoc because neither positivity nor overlap degree is the actual variance.
seed_details.rebuttal: A defensible version needs the HT variance upper bound from the CLT kernel to certify the surrogate objective, which makes it a second-stage project rather than the first angle.

proposed_from.literature_map:
Aronow and Samii (2017) define the design-plus-exposure-mapping HT architecture and make first and joint exposure probabilities the bottleneck for randomization-based inference.
Ugander, Karrer, Backstrom, and Kleinberg (2013) compute exact exposure probabilities for selected graph-cluster exposure rules and show variance is the real design problem, but explicitly leave tractable variance objectives, continuous exposure structure, and misspecification analysis open.
Chin (2018) shows Stein's method can deliver CLTs under interference when dependency degree is controlled, suggesting a route once the ego-cluster dependency graph is derived from primitives rather than assumed.
Leung (2019/2022) weakens exact neighborhood interference to ANI and proves approximate normality plus HAC-bias results, but in a superpopulation network-growth framework rather than fixed finite-population ego-cluster inference.
Gao and Ding (2023) recover Hajek/WLS equivalence and show regression-based network-robust covariances can be anti-conservative, sharpening the variance-estimation obstacle after a CLT is available.
Liu, Hu, and Zhang (2026) are the closest ego-cluster paper: they obtain model-based consistency, asymptotic normality, and variance-guided clustering, then explicitly list directed networks, adaptive randomization, and more general interference as future directions.
Aronow, Eckles, Samii, and Zonszein (2021) survey randomized-experiment spillover methods and keep design probabilities and exposure misspecification at center stage.
Sävje, Aronow, and Hudgens (2021) show standard estimators can still target generalized average effects under limited unknown interference, but also warn that conventional confidence statements can fail.
The unresolved load-bearing gap for ego-cluster experiments is the conjunction of three pieces: computable pi_i and pi_ij for analyst-chosen local exposure rules, a dependency graph stated in overlap counts rather than abstract local dependence, and a conservative variance theory that does not assume away missing cross-potential terms.
The strongest cold-start angle is therefore an M12 kernel: exact incidence-polynomial exposure algebra feeding an overlap-derived HT CLT, with conservative variance kept as an explicit open-ended question rather than smuggled in as a routine theorem.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: thm:ego-overlap-clt
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: The Wald interval is oracle because it uses sigma_n rather than an observed conservative variance estimator.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: The theorem is only an oracle design-based CLT/coverage benchmark under the stated primitive overlap, positivity, boundedness, and nondegenerate-variance conditions.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: ass:primitive-degree-rate
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: The CLT is conditional on the primitive overlap frontier 2 q_n kappa_n (omega_n - 1) = o(n^(1/4)); it does not claim validity for arbitrary ego-cluster reuse.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: The headline CLT is bound to the bounded-overlap sparse-ego regime.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: oeq:conservative-variance
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: The computable conservative variance envelope is explicitly open and no operational non-oracle confidence interval is claimed.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: Non-oracle coverage is only a solve target until Vbar_n and its sharpness/nontriviality properties are constructed.

banked_reason: D0.5.G tier=subfield < field floor: the HT CLT is an application of Chin's dependency-graph Stein CLT after exact ego-incidence exposure-probability bookkeeping, and the feasible-Wald sharpness is tautological within the completion-envelope class — sound and useful infrastructure but not a field-level new rate, necessity theorem, estimator-class optimum, or primitive characterization.
